surface: GET /api/verify-signal?hash=<signal_hash> (the form baked into every webhook verify_url) — dual with the existing ?signalId=<int>
serving: Caddy proxies /api/verify-signal → app:3000. The /verify landing page (static, Caddy file_server) reads ?hash= and calls this endpoint. R2 (Mr.1-ratified): no Caddyfile change.
naming_law: public response uses calls-language; verdict surfaced as both `call` (NAMING-LAW) and `direction` (existing render-compat); price as both `price_at_call` and `price`. `signal_hash` kept (existing identifier).

pending_200.when: call recorded but not yet in a Merkle batch

forbidden_key_rationale: Phase-E internal outcome metrics (Data Integrity LAW). getSignalByHash's SELECT is the allow-list — it never projects these even when the underlying signals row has them populated (asserted by tests/verify-hash.test.ts).
existing_path_invariant: GET /api/verify-signal?signalId=<int> response is byte-for-byte unchanged (signal:{id,coin,direction,confidence,timeframe,price,timestamp,hash}); only the new ?hash= branch adds call/exchange/regime/price_at_call.
drift_check_command: H=$(curl -s 'https://algovault.com/api/merkle-batches' | jq -r '.batches[0]' >/dev/null; echo 0x...); curl -s "https://algovault.com/api/verify-signal?hash=$KNOWN_HASH" | jq 'keys, .signal|keys'; echo 'assert: no key matches ^(outcome_|pfe_|mae_|return_pct_|price_after_)'; curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' "https://algovault.com/api/verify-signal?hash=0x$(printf 'f%.0s' {1..64})"  # expect 404
